title: Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary
tagline: Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Bihar

overview.short: Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary is a protected area and bird sanctuary located in the Darbhanga district in the Indian state of Bihar.
overview.description: Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary is a protected area and bird sanctuary located in the Darbhanga district in the Indian state of Bihar. The sanctuary is named after the nearby Kusheshwar Asthan temple, a prominent Hindu pilgrimage site dedicated to Lord Shiva.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (125 km) and the nearest railway station is Muzaffarpur Junction (97 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kusheshwar Asthan Bird Sanctuary?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kusheshwar Asthan, Uda, Bihar.
